title: Pre-Award — Proposal Components
content:
Technical components typically required for a research proposal at Morgan State University. ORA emphasizes that each Funding Opportunity Announcement (FoA) has specific requirements, so applicants must thoroughly review the relevant FoA before drafting. Contact ORA at ask.ora@morgan.edu or 443-885-4044 (fax 443-885-8280) for assistance.

Standard proposal components:

Abstract / Executive Summary: A concise summary written within sponsor-specified space limits, covering the problem, objectives, approach, and project cost. The abstract should be clear and should accurately reflect and parallel the content of the proposal.

Introduction / Background: Demonstrates knowledge of the field, identifies research gaps, and establishes project importance while relating specific aims to broader long-term goals.

Goals & Objectives: Specifies broad long-term goals and expected outcomes, followed by clear, brief, realistic, and measurable specific objectives.

Methodology: Explains how objectives will be achieved, relating the proposed approach to specific objectives and justifying the selection of methods.

Key Personnel: Describes qualifications, relevant experience, and specific roles of all key personnel. List the Principal Investigator first. Include any personnel critical to project success even if their salary is not being requested from the sponsor.

Evaluation: For training or demonstration projects, the evaluation section relates evaluation methods to stated objectives and describes data collection, timelines, resources, and intended use of results.
